turns.student: Hi, I'm here to help you. What's been going on?
turns.patient: I've had this terrible cough for five days now. It started out dry and just wouldn't go away, but then it turned yellowish-green and I've been bringing up mucus nonstop.

turns.student: That sounds really uncomfortable. I can tell this has been weighing on you.
turns.patient: To be honest, it's not just the cough that's bothering me - I'm worried about how long it's taking to get better. The dextromethorphan hasn't done much for me at all, and I'm getting a bit concerned.

turns.student: I want to make sure we don't miss anything important. When these episodes happen, does your body react in any other way?
turns.patient: I've been experiencing some difficulty breathing and wheezing when I exert myself or even just move around a lot. It's like my chest is tight and I get this weird whooshing sound when I breathe out.

turns.student: I know it can be scary to think about, but do you notice any sweating or feeling faint during these episodes?
turns.patient: I've actually been having night sweats, soaking through my pajamas every night for 4 nights now. It's really unsettling, and I'm getting a bit worried it might be related to something more serious than just a cold.

turns.student: Thank you for sharing that. I really appreciate your honesty. Does the discomfort stay in one place or does it spread anywhere?
turns.patient: To be honest, it feels like the tightness and wheezing are mostly confined to my chest area, but sometimes it radiates up into my neck and shoulders. I've also noticed that when I wake up from those night sweats, my throat often feels really sore and dry.

turns.student: It must be frightening when symptoms like these happen. Any other areas of your body affected?
turns.patient: To be honest, I haven't really thought about any other areas being affected. But, I do have seasonal allergies, so I've been taking cetirizine on an as-needed basis during this time, and I'm wondering if that might be related to some of my symptoms or not.

turns.student: I'm concerned about you. Have you noticed any swelling anywhere? Even minor changes?
turns.patient: To be honest, I haven't really noticed any noticeable swelling anywhere on my body, but I do have a bit of a rash on my arm from an allergic reaction to penicillin that happened a few years ago.

turns.student: Sometimes when people are going through something like this, they also feel nauseous or have stomach upset. Has that happened to you?
turns.patient: I haven't really experienced any nausea or stomach issues directly related to my cough and symptoms. I did miss a few days of work due to fatigue and malaise, but I'm not sure if that's directly connected to what we're discussing now.

turns.student: I can see this is taking a toll on you. How has your energy been? Your appetite?
turns.patient: My energy levels have been really low - I've missed three days of work because of fatigue and malaise, and even when I'm at home, I just feel so drained all the time. As for my appetite, to be honest, it's taken a hit too - food hasn't really appealed to me much over the past few days, and I've been struggling to keep anything down.

turns.student: Have you had any fevers or night sweats? I know it might seem unrelated, but it helps me understand the full picture.
turns.patient: Yes, I've had a low-grade fever, around 38.2°C (100.8°F), that's been intermittent, and it's worse in the evenings. And like I mentioned earlier, the night sweats have been really bad - soaking through my pajamas every night for 4 nights now.

turns.student: Your health really matters to me as your care provider. Can you tell me about any other medical conditions you've been diagnosed with?
turns.patient: To be honest, I'm not sure how this is all connected, but aside from my seasonal allergies and that penicillin allergy rash a few years ago, everything was normal at my last physical exam about a year ago. But to be frank, I've been trying to keep up with what's going on in class because several students have been sick with upper respiratory infections in the past month, and since I work with young children daily as an elementary school teacher... (pauses, looking worried)

turns.student: I know medications can be a hassle. Are you taking everything as prescribed, or has anything been hard to keep up with?
turns.patient: I've been trying to stick to the dextromethorphan for my cough, but like I mentioned earlier, it hasn't really been doing much for me. To be honest, it's been a bit of a struggle to remember to take it regularly, especially since I'm not feeling well in the first place.

turns.student: I don't want to pry, but understanding your lifestyle really helps me help you. How do you eat on a typical day?
turns.patient: I'm a bit worried about being a "germ vector" since I work with kids all day, but if it helps figure out what's going on... Typically, I try to eat a balanced diet, but lately, my appetite has been really off. When I do feel like eating, I usually have some toast or oatmeal for breakfast, maybe a sandwich or leftovers for lunch, and then something light in the evening. But honestly, even that feels too much effort most days.

turns.student: It sounds like your life keeps you really busy. Do you get a chance to exercise or stay active?
turns.patient: Honestly, it's been really tough lately. With missing work and feeling so drained, I haven't even had the energy for my usual walks around the block with my partner. We usually try to take a short walk after dinner, but even that feels like too much exertion right now.

turns.student: I hate to ask this, but it's really important — has anyone in your family had serious health problems?
turns.patient: My mom actually has asthma. She's been managing it with medication for years, but sometimes it can be a bit of a challenge. I've always been worried about inheriting something like that, especially since I'm experiencing some similar respiratory issues now.

turns.student: I'm so sorry to hear that. That must have been incredibly difficult. Do you worry about your own health because of it?
turns.patient: Yes, it does play on my mind a lot, especially when I see my mom struggling with asthma and now me experiencing these similar symptoms. It's hard not to wonder if there might be some connection or if I'm at risk for something similar.

turns.student: Given everything you've been through, I want to be thorough. Do you drink alcohol? How do you cope with stress?
turns.patient: I do have a glass of wine on weekends, usually just one or two. As for coping with stress, to be honest, it's been really tough lately. I've always tried to stay active and exercise as a way to manage stress, but like I said earlier, that's not really been an option for me right now. Lately, I've been trying to talk to my partner about how I'm feeling, and we've also been trying to take some time together just to relax and unwind, even if it's just sitting on the couch watching a movie or something like that.

turns.student: I want to make sure we're being thorough. Do you smoke or use any substances?
turns.patient: No, I've never smoked. And to be honest, aside from the occasional glass of wine on weekends, I don't really use any other substances regularly.

turns.student: Is there anything you've been hesitant to tell me? I promise there's no judgment here.
turns.patient: I've been trying to be as open and honest as possible, but...I guess one thing that's been on my mind is that I'm a teacher, and I work with kids all day. There have been several of them in my class who've come down with upper respiratory infections in the past month, and I'm worried that maybe I picked something up from them or vice versa? It feels a bit like I'm a "germ vector" or something, but I don't know if that's relevant or not.

turns.student: Before we wrap up, is there anything else you're worried about that you want to share?
turns.patient: To be honest, what's been on my mind lately is that recurrent wheezing episodes I've had since childhood. They used to happen 2-3 times a year with colds or exercise, but never really got formally diagnosed. It's hard not to wonder if there might be some connection between those and what I'm experiencing now.
